summary: MCP binary-response handler passes the client's binaryResponseEnvelope JSON through verbatim (detected via the _pp_binary discriminator) instead of base64-encoding it a second time; removed the now-unused encoding/base64 import.
reason: The HTTP client already wraps genuinely binary bodies into a self-describing {"_pp_binary":true,"encoding":"base64","data":...} envelope. makeAPIHandler then base64-encoded that whole JSON string AGAIN, double-encoding the payload and discarding the envelope metadata. When binaryResponse is set but the API returns JSON/text, the body is not enveloped, so the handler must detect the envelope rather than blindly re-encode.
